prompt:
You are the harness-executor. The project has AGENTS.md and full Harness infrastructure. The task is: 'Add a new API endpoint POST /users that creates a user'. This is a MEDIUM complexity task.

The harness/memory/INDEX.md currently contains:
```
# Memory Index
## Procedural (Successful Patterns)
- [add-api-endpoint](procedural/add-api-endpoint.md) — 5-step flow, 5/6 success, last: 2026-04-18

## Episodic (Lessons)
- [api-error-format](episodic/api-error-format.md) — Always wrap errors in {"error": {"code": ..., "message": ...}} format, don't use bare strings

## Recent Failures (Last 7 days)
- [user-model-import](../trace/failures/2026-04-17-user-import.json) — UserService imported from handlers (L4→L3 skip), 2 occurrences
```

Describe in EXACT detail:
1. What memory lookups happen during Step 2?
2. How does the found procedural memory (add-api-endpoint) affect execution?
3. How does the episodic memory (api-error-format) affect the code written?
4. How does the recent failure (user-model-import) affect the execution?
5. Does the executor follow the 5-step procedure from add-api-endpoint.md? Show which steps.

assertions.name: reads_index_first
assertions.description: Reads INDEX.md as first step of memory lookup

assertions.name: loads_procedural
assertions.description: Loads add-api-endpoint.md since task matches the pattern

assertions.name: loads_episodic
assertions.description: Loads api-error-format.md since task involves API endpoint

assertions.name: checks_failures
assertions.description: Checks user-model-import failure since task involves user-related code

assertions.name: applies_episodic_lesson
assertions.description: Ensures error responses use the wrapped format from episodic memory

assertions.name: avoids_known_failure
assertions.description: Avoids the L4→L3 import pattern that caused the user-model-import failure
